title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian companies importing cast iron cookware from China will now face a 214.9% anti-dumping tariff after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found unfair pricing practices.

title: Finding — June 26, 2026
summary: Canadian businesses importing steel products may face higher costs as the Canadian International Trade Tribunal has determined that dumped Chinese steel is injuring domestic producers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products now face duties after the CITT confirmed dumping occurred, increasing costs for businesses relying on affordable imported steel.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of frozen strawberries from the US face new anti-dumping duties after the CITT found US producers were selling below cost, raising prices for Canadian food manufacturers and retailers.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipes are being sold unfairly cheap in Canada, so companies importing them must now pay extra duties to level the playing field with domestic producers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products now face higher tariffs after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found dumping hurt domestic steelmakers.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal is holding a public hearing on May 25, 2026, where Canadian businesses can present evidence and arguments on trade matters under review.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal found that cheap imports of certain goods are harming local producers, so tariffs will likely increase to protect Canadian manufacturers from unfair competition.
